objections.description: Architectural contradiction: ActionSheet is defined as a lazy-loaded route ('/document/:id/action') but specified to render as a BottomSheet on mobile and Modal on desktop. BottomSheets and Modals are overlay patterns that require the underlying DocumentScreen to remain mounted and visible underneath. Implementing this as a route will unmount the DocumentScreen, breaking the visual stacking context and back-button behavior. Users expect to dismiss a sheet to return to the underlying document, not navigate 'back' to a fresh document load.
objections.suggested_fix: Change ActionSheet from a route to a query param state (e.g., ?action=compose) or hash fragment (#action) within DocumentScreen. DocumentScreen should conditionally render ActionSheet as an overlay. Alternatively, use a modal route pattern where DocumentScreen renders via <Outlet /> or similar when child route is active, preserving the document view in the background.

objections.description: No specification for navigation direction detection (forward vs. back). The motion system defines slideForward and slideBack animations keyed to navigation direction, but the router implementation provides no mechanism to distinguish between entering a screen via deep link/forward navigation vs. returning via back button. Without history state management or location.key comparison, all navigation will appear as forward animations, creating disorienting UX when using browser back/forward gestures.
objections.suggested_fix: Implement a NavigationDirectionProvider that tracks location history stack. Compare current location.key with previous to determine direction. Pass direction ('forward' | 'back') as prop to animated screen wrappers, or use AnimatePresence with custom key logic to trigger correct exit/enter animations.

objections.description: Search state persistence across navigation is undefined. When a user searches on SearchScreen, clicks a result to DocumentScreen, then clicks back, the SearchScreen will remount (it's lazy-loaded) and lose query input, filters, and scroll position. This violates the 'back button returns to previous state' expectation and forces users to re-enter search terms.
objections.suggested_fix: Move search query state from SearchScreen local state to SearchProvider (global) or persist in sessionStorage. Ensure SearchScreen restores query and scroll position on remount. Alternatively, use React Router's useBlocker or state persistence hooks, or keep SearchScreen mounted in background when navigating to child routes.

objections.description: Primary navigation structure is undefined. The spec mentions TabBar (mobile) and SideRail (desktop) but lists no navigation items. Given the routes include Dashboard, Search, Document, and Settings, there's an IA conflict: Search appears both as a function within Dashboard and as a standalone route. Without explicit tab definitions, developers may implement conflicting navigation patterns (e.g., Search tab vs Search field in Dashboard).
objections.suggested_fix: Explicitly define navigation items: Dashboard (Home), Library/History (if applicable), Settings. Search should be a persistent search bar in the Shell header, not a tab, to avoid mode confusion. Document the information hierarchy: Search is a function available globally; Dashboard is the landing state; Document is a detail view; Settings is a configuration space.

objections.description: No explicit 'Up' or 'Back to results' navigation specified in DocumentScreen. Users entering from deep link have no way to reach SearchScreen except browser back (which fails if no history exists). Users entering from SearchScreen need clear affordance to return to results list, not just browser chrome.
objections.suggested_fix: Add ScreenHeader with contextual back button. If document was accessed from search (pass state via location.state), button reads 'Back to Results' and returns to /search. If accessed via deep link/direct entry, button navigates to / (Dashboard). Implement useNavigationSource hook to determine entry point.

suggestions.description: Add explicit focus management for route transitions. When navigating to a new screen, focus should move to the screen title (h1) or first interactive element. When opening ActionSheet, focus should trap within the sheet. When closing, focus should return to the trigger element. Currently, focus behavior during SPA navigation is undefined, creating accessibility gaps for screen reader users.

Implement skeleton states for progressive loading. The DocumentScreen fetches document data, but the spec shows TimelineList without loading states. Since documents may have long timelines, implement a staggered skeleton that matches the eventual content structure (header skeleton first, timeline items skeleton below) rather than generic ScreenSkeleton to reduce layout shift and perceived load time.

Clarify the 4-state renderer states. The spec mentions 'Generic 4-state renderer' but doesn't enumerate them. Explicitly define: (1) Loading (skeleton), (2) Empty (no data), (3) Error (fetch failed), (4) Success (data display). Ensure each state has defined transitions and that Error states include retry affordances.
